chapter_context.summary: Absalom conspires, steals the hearts of the people, and launches a massive rebellion; David and his loyal followers flee Jerusalem in tears.

outline.text: Absalom Steals the Hearts of the Men of Israel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Absalom Steals the Hearts of the Men of Israel (15:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Absalom Obtains Leave for Hebron; Launches Open Rebellion
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Absalom Obtains Leave for Hebron; Launches Open Rebellion (15:7-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: David Escapes Jerusalem; Ittai's Beautiful Vow of Loyalty
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Escapes Jerusalem; Ittai's Beautiful Vow of Loyalty (15:13-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: David Sends the Ark and Priests Back to Jerusalem
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Sends the Ark and Priests Back to Jerusalem (15:24-29), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: David Prays Against Ahithophel; Sends Hushai back to Spy
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Prays Against Ahithophel; Sends Hushai back to Spy (15:30-37), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

key_themes.title: The Davidic Covenant
key_themes.description: God's unconditional promise of an eternal dynasty, ultimately pointing forward to the Messiah.

key_themes.title: Sin and Consequences
key_themes.description: While God offers complete forgiveness for sincere repentance, the earthly consequences of sin remain.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 15 demonstrate the grace of God in the midst of human failure?
What lessons about leadership and accountability can you apply to your own life from this chapter?

cultural_historical.title: Jerusalem as Capital
cultural_historical.text: By capturing Jerusalem, a neutral city on the border of Judah and Benjamin, David strategically united the fractured northern and southern tribes under a single capital.
